mechanism_narrative: Proximity of the federal funds rate to the zero lower bound reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, providing a structural floor for S&P 500 valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in > 65% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0% signals persistent structural labor market weakness, which suppresses consumer spending growth and forces downward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE remains above 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts serve as a leading indicator for broad economic activity; a sustained upward trend in housing construction signals recovering demand and supports positive equity momentum.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between HOUST momentum and 60-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where consumer price inflation accelerates while real GDP growth decelerates creates stagflationary pressure, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between rising CPIAUCSL and falling GDPC1 fails to predict negative returns (interaction coefficient not significant at 95% confidence).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, rising industrial production directly correlates with increasing capacity utilization and profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum shows no predictive power for 20-day returns during the identified recovery regime (IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll additions exceeding 150,000 indicate robust labor demand and immediate income growth, boosting consumer confidence and generating short-term positive equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are negative in > 60% of months where PAYEMS increases by more than 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: Real personal spending is the primary driver of corporate revenues; positive momentum in real spending directly predicts positive equity returns over the intermediate horizon.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth rate is negatively correlated with 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year inflation exceeding 2.5% triggers expectations of monetary tightening or margin compression, negatively impacting equity valuations regardless of the current policy rate level.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in > 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 2.5%.
